level1_segments.summary: The head surgeon performs initial patient contact and a brief cutting action with short pauses in between.
The head surgeon engages in a continuous sequence of drilling the patient while holding the drill.
The head surgeon transitions from touching the patient to holding and manipulating a surgical instrument with brief pauses.
The head surgeon performs a continuous sequence of bone sawing and instrument handling on the patient, including brief pauses and interactions with the robotic arm.
The head surgeon performs an initial bone cut using a bone saw and briefly pauses.
The surgeon switches to a surgical instrument and interacts with the Mako robotic arm while sawing and touching the patient.
The head surgeon resumes sawing the patient for an extended period.
The surgeon drills into the patient and then holds the drill.
The surgeon touches the patient, holds a surgical instrument, and continues touching the patient.

level2_segments.summary: The head surgeon performs initial patient contact and begins the bone preparation process with drilling.
The surgeon transitions to manipulating instruments and performs extensive bone sawing and robotic arm interactions.
The head surgeon executes a focused sequence of bone cutting and sawing while coordinating with the robotic system.
The surgeon concludes the bone work phase by drilling and performing final instrument handling and patient contact.

level1_segments.summary: The surgical instrument is repeatedly transferred between the circulator and scrub nurses during an extended preparation and staging phase.
The instrument is held by the robot technician and scrub nurse, then passed to the circulator and back to the scrub nurse for further handling.
The head surgeon takes possession of the instrument, uses it with the assistant surgeon, and exchanges it with the scrub nurse during active surgical manipulation.
The head surgeon briefly retrieves and uses the instrument again after a short pause, with the scrub nurse assisting in the handoff.
The surgical instrument is held by the scrub nurse, briefly transferred to the assistant surgeon and circulator nurse for use, and then returned to the scrub nurse for the remainder of the period.

level2_segments.summary: The surgical instrument undergoes an extended preparation and staging phase involving repeated transfers between the circulator, scrub nurse, and robot technician before active use.
The head surgeon actively utilizes the instrument for surgical manipulation with assistance from the assistant surgeon and scrub nurse during two distinct usage intervals.
The instrument is primarily held by the scrub nurse with brief transfers to the assistant surgeon and circulator for limited use before being returned to storage.

level1_segments.summary: The robot technician briefly assists the scrub nurse after an initial idle period.
The technician interacts with the MPS workstation and touches the robotic arm before pausing.
The technician performs a series of manipulations on the MPS workstation interspersed with short idle periods.
The technician calibrates and manipulates the Mako robotic arm with intermittent pauses.
The technician performs final manipulations on the Mako robotic arm followed by a long idle period.
The robot technician briefly manipulates the Mako robotic arm after a period of inactivity.
The technician holds a surgical instrument and then performs two brief interactions with the MPS workstation separated by short pauses.
The robot technician assists the assistant surgeon before returning to a long period of inactivity.
The technician alternates between manipulating the Mako robotic arm and the infrared tracker with brief pauses in between.
The technician interacts with the MPS workstation, manipulates the robotic arm, touches the surgical drape, and finally operates the C-arm.

level2_segments.summary: The robot technician performs initial setup tasks, including assisting the scrub nurse and configuring the MPS workstation.
The technician focuses on calibrating and manipulating the Mako robotic arm to prepare it for the procedure.
The technician handles surgical instruments and provides direct assistance to the assistant surgeon.
The technician actively manages the robotic arm and infrared tracker while coordinating with the C-arm and workstation during the main surgical phase.

level1_segments.summary: The scrub nurse prepares the instrument table and handles surgical instruments with brief pauses.
The scrub nurse manipulates and calibrates the Mako robotic arm with brief pauses in between.
The scrub nurse alternates between holding instruments, cleaning, and preparing the patient while waiting for the next phase.
The scrub nurse actively assists the surgical team by preparing the instrument table, holding instruments, and supporting both the head and assistant surgeons.
The scrub nurse assists the head surgeon and prepares the instrument table while holding surgical instruments.
The scrub nurse holds a surgical instrument for an extended period.
The scrub nurse assists the head surgeon.
The scrub nurse prepares the instrument table.
The scrub nurse alternates between holding instruments, preparing the table, and assisting the head surgeon with brief pauses in between.
The scrub nurse holds instruments, prepares the patient, and touches the patient with brief interruptions for table preparation.

level2_segments.summary: The scrub nurse prepares the instrument table and calibrates the robotic arm for the procedure.
The scrub nurse actively assists the surgical team by holding instruments, preparing the table, and supporting the surgeons during the main operative phase.
The scrub nurse continues to support the procedure by managing the instrument table, holding tools, and assisting with patient preparation.

level1_segments.summary: The robot monitor is initialized and finalized in two consecutive cycles.
A comprehensive pre-surgery verification sequence is performed, including arm status, homing, accuracy, brake, and angle checks, concluding with a successful status confirmation.

The system performs an extended bone registration process covering femoral and tibial landmarks, including specific views for distal, posterior, anterior, and bevel surfaces, before finalizing the registration.

level2_segments.summary: The robot monitor undergoes initialization and a comprehensive pre-surgery verification sequence to confirm system readiness.
The system performs an extended bone registration process covering femoral and tibial landmarks to finalize the surgical plan.

level1_segments.summary: The robot is powered on to initiate the setup process.
Initial calibration of the robot is performed by the MPS.
The robot is dressed for sterility, followed by the installation and removal of calibration arrays and final calibration by the nurse.

level2_segments.summary: The robot undergoes a comprehensive setup process including power-on, initial calibration, and sterile dressing with final calibration adjustments.
